hero.title: JOSÉ ANTONIO MORENO MARÍN
hero.subtitle: Full Stack Developer
hero.description: Full Stack Developer with a strong focus on Backend using Java and Spring Boot. Specialized in building scalable and efficient solutions, bringing value to every phase of the software development lifecycle with exceptional problem-solving adaptability.

profile.title: Professional Profile
profile.description: Full stack developer with experience in Angular, Java, Spring Boot, TypeScript, and Laravel. I quickly integrate into new projects and teams, offering a different perspective thanks to my background in physiotherapy. I stand out for my ability to communicate complex technical concepts in a clear and accessible way, facilitating collaboration between technical and non-technical teams. Always committed to continuous learning and delivering quality code, which I now apply to full-stack development.

experience.title: Work Experience
experience.label: Professional Experience
responsibilities: Development and maintenance of mobile applications using Angular, TypeScript, Laravel and Ionic
Built REST APIs in Laravel for business logic and data management
Collaboration with designers and other developers to create intuitive interfaces

skills.title: Technical and Soft Skills

projects.title: My Projects
projects.label: My Work
projects.subtitle: Here are some projects I've worked on. Feel free to explore them.
projects.project3Description: A backend API for product management built with Java and Spring Boot. Features category management, full CRUD operations and API documentation with Swagger. Uses MySQL for data persistence.
projects.project4Description: A REST API for powerbuilding and fitness coaching management. Features a rich domain model (avoiding the anemic model anti-pattern), zero-trust validations and secure authentication with JWT. Currently in development, with domain models and unit tests already implemented.

transition.title: From Physiotherapy to Code
transition.subtitle: My journey as a physiotherapist has shaped me with a unique set of soft skills that are highly valuable in the corporate software development world.
transition.card1Text: In physiotherapy, I developed diagnostic skills to identify problems, develop treatment plans, and assess progress. Similarly, I use these skills to debug code, analyze system requirements and design effective solutions.
transition.card2Text: Worked as part of multidisciplinary healthcare teams, requiring effective communication, collaboration and multitasking. This prepared me to work in software development teams with diverse roles and backgrounds.
transition.card3Text: Managed patient records, scheduled appointments, tracked progress, and handled billing. Developed strong organizational skills that translate to software project management, version control, and documentation.
transition.card4Text: Managed treatment equipment, followed detailed protocols, and applied scientific methods. These skills easily transferred to programming, working with APIs, debugging code, and following software architecture.
transition.card5Text: Physiotherapy required staying up to date on the latest methods and certifications. In software development, I maintain this same commitment to continuous learning amidst a constantly evolving technological landscape.
